memoryArtifacts.headline: tree_Moores_Ancestry
memoryArtifacts.description: This document was compiled by Orville Sullivan L6L2-JJB who is a grandson of Carrie Moore and Delos Sullivan, I was given a paper copy and decided to scan it in and create the searchable text form of the document so others may see and use it. I  tagged a number of the people found in the document to make it easier for others to locate.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Johannes Conrad Moore於1791年10月27日出生在德國貝也普法爾茲時，他的父親Joh. Daniel Mohr24歲，母親Anna Maria Nagelin21歲。他於1823年3月2日與Sussanna Elisabetha Nagell在美國賓夕法尼亞州勒班農喬內斯托結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1850年住在美國賓夕法尼亞州威斯特摩蘭戴利且於1860年住在美國賓夕法尼亞州威斯特摩蘭戴利 堂史普。他在美國賓夕法尼亞州威斯特摩蘭被列為cooper。他去世於1863年在美國賓夕法尼亞州威斯特摩蘭尤斯托享年72歲，埋葬在美國賓夕法尼亞州威斯特摩蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Johannes Conrad Moore于1791年10月27日出生在德国貝也普法爾茲时，他的父亲Joh. Daniel Mohr24岁，母亲Anna Maria Nagelin21岁。他于1823年3月2日与Sussanna Elisabetha Nagell在美国宾夕法尼亚州勒班農喬內斯托结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他于1850年住在美国宾夕法尼亚州威斯特摩蘭戴利且于1860年住在美国宾夕法尼亚州威斯特摩蘭戴利 堂史普。他在美国宾夕法尼亚州威斯特摩蘭被列为cooper。他去世于1863年在美国宾夕法尼亚州威斯特摩蘭尤斯托享年72岁，埋葬在美国宾夕法尼亚州威斯特摩蘭。

nameMeanings.name: Moore
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Moor
meanings: English, Irish, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Moore  .
German: variant of   Mohr  .

Dutch (also De Moor): nickname for a man of swarthy complexion, from Middle Dutch moor ‘Moor’ (from Latin maurus), or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a Moor. Compare De Moor .

variants.name: More
meanings: French: nickname for a dark-skinned man, from a diminutive of Old French more ‘Moor’ (see More ).

variants.name: Morrin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Morin, a diminutive of More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus); see Moore 2 and 3.
English: perhaps sometimes from the Middle English personal name Morwin (Old English Mōrwine, the first element of which is uncertain and the second wine ‘friend’).

variants.name: Mauro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Domenic, Giuseppe, Antonio, Carmine, Carmelo, Domenico, Gaetano, Nino, Pasquale.

Italian (mainly southern): from the personal name Mauro, derived from the Latin personal and ethnic name Maurus, borne by various early Christian saints and meaning ‘Moor, North African’. Compare Moore and Moro .

variants.name: Moresco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Primo, Gino, Luciano, Riccardo.
Italian:
(Morésco): from a medieval personal name or a nickname for someone from North Africa or a swarthy or dark-haired man, from medieval Latin Mauriscus ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2).

variants.name: Morey
meanings: Irish (Limerick and Cork): variant of   Moore   4.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Mory, a pet form of Amauri (see Amery ).
English: perhaps also a variant of   Moore   1–3.

variants.name: Morrell
meanings: English (northern): from the medieval personal name Morel, a diminutive of Middle English or Old French More. Compare Moore .
Americanized form of French   Morel  . Compare   Morell   3.

variants.name: Moores
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): variant of Moore , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McMoore
meanings: Irish: variant of Moore 4, in which Mac- ‘son of’ has been prefixed in modern times to give the name (Ó Mórdha ‘descendant of Mórdha’) an unmistakably Gaelic identity.

variants.name: Moro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Mario, Alberto, Jorge, Lino, Luis, Luiz, Manuel, Ramiro, Adolfo, Aida.  Italian Angelo, Pino, Pio, Remo, Antonio, Ciro, Cosmo, Elio, Enrico, Geno, Giancarlo, Gino.
Spanish: nickname or ethnic name from moro ‘Moor’, from Latin Maurus ‘Moor, North African’.
Italian: nickname from moro ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2, compare 1 above), also ‘black-haired man’.

nameMeanings.name: Johannes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

nameMeanings.name: Conrad
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual English spelling of Konrad, a Germanic personal name derived from kuon ‘bold’ + rad ‘counsel’. It was used occasionally in Britain in the Middle Ages in honour of a 10th-century bishop of Constance, but modern use in the English-speaking world is a reimportation from Germany dating mainly from the 19th century.

historicEvents.title: Creating the Eleventh Amendment
historicEvents.description: The Eleventh Amendment restricts the ability of any people to start a lawsuit against the states in federal court.

historicEvents.title: Movement to Washington D.C.
historicEvents.description: While the growth of the new nation was exponential, the United States didn’t have permanent location to house the Government. The First capital was temporary in New York City but by the second term of George Washington the Capital moved to Philadelphia for the following 10 years. Ultimately during the Presidency of John Adams, the Capital found a permanent home in the District of Columbia.

historicEvents.title: Harrisburg Becomes the State Capital
historicEvents.description: Harrisburg had important parts with migration, the Civil War, and the Industrial Revolution.
historicEvents.urlTitle: United States History-History of Harrisburg Pennsylvania

historicEvents.title: Germans defeat Napolean in Leipzig
historicEvents.description: On Oct 19, 1813, Germany defeats Napolean. The commanding officer for the Allied side was Prince Karl Phillipp Schwarzenberg.

historicEvents.title: The Second Great Awakening
historicEvents.description: Being a second spiritual and religious awakening, like the First Great Awakening, many Churches began to spring up from other denominations. Many people began to rapidly join the Baptist and Methodist congregations. Many converts to these religions believed that the Awakening was the precursor of a new millennial age.

historicEvents.title: The Oregon Trail
historicEvents.description: Many people started their 2,170-mile West trek to settle the land found by Louis and Clark. They used large-wheeled wagons to pack most of their belongings and were guided by trails that were made by the previous trappers and traders who walked the area. Over time the trail needed annual improvements to make the trip faster and safer. Most of Interstate 80 and 84 cover most of the ground that was the original trail.

historicEvents.title: The Book of Mormon Published in German
historicEvents.description: On May 25, 1852, the Book of Mormon is published in German.
historicEvents.urlTitle: The Book of Mormon Published in German

historicEvents.title: Battle of Gettysburg
historicEvents.description: The three day Battle of Gettysburg was one of the bloodiest of the American Civil War. Between the Confederates and Unions, somewhere between 46,000 and 51,000 people died that day.

en.lifeSummary: When Johannes Conrad Moore was born on 27 October 1791, in Palatinate, Bavaria, Germany, his father, Joh. Daniel Mohr, was 24 and his mother, Anna Maria Nagelin, was 21. He married Sussanna Elisabetha Nagell on 2 March 1823, in Jonestown, Lebanon, Pennsylvania, United States. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He lived in Derry, Westmoreland, Pennsylvania, United States in 1850 and Derry Township, Westmoreland, Pennsylvania, United States in 1860. His occupation is listed as cooper in Westmoreland, Pennsylvania, United States. He died in 1863, in Youngstown, Westmoreland, Pennsylvania, United States, at the age of 72, and was buried in Westmoreland, Pennsylvania, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Johannes Conrad Moore est né le 27 octobre 1791, à Pfalz, Bayern, Allemagne, son père, Joh. Daniel Mohr, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Anna Maria Nagelin, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Sussanna Elisabetha Nagell le 2 mars 1823, à Jonestown, Lebanon, Pennsylvanie, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il a vécu à Derry, Westmoreland, Pennsylvanie, États-Unis en 1850 et à Derry Township, Westmoreland, Pennsylvanie, États-Unis en 1860. Sa profession de son indiquée est cooper à Westmoreland, Pennsylvanie, États-Unis. Il est décédé en 1863, à Youngstown, Westmoreland, Pennsylvanie, États-Unis, à l’âge de 72 ans, et a été enterré à Westmoreland, Pennsylvanie, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Johannes Conrad Moore am 27. Oktober 1791 in Pfalz, Bayern, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Joh. Daniel Mohr 24 und seine Mutter Anna Maria Nagelin 21. Er hat am 2. März 1823 in Jonestown, Lebanon, Pennsylvania, Vereinigte Staaten Sussanna Elisabetha Nagell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. 1850 hat er in Derry, Westmoreland, Pennsylvania, Vereinigte Staaten und 1860 in Derry Township, Westmoreland, Pennsylvania, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt. sein Beruf ist als cooper in Westmoreland, Pennsylvania, Vereinigte Staaten aufgeführt. Er starb 1863 im Alter von 72 Jahren in Youngstown, Westmoreland, Pennsylvania, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in Westmoreland, Pennsylvania, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Johannes Conrad Moore è nato il il 27 ottobre 1791 nel Pfalz, Bayern, Germania, il suo padre, Joh. Daniel Mohr, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Anna Maria Nagelin, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Sussanna Elisabetha Nagell il 2 marzo 1823, nel Jonestown, Lebanon, Pennsylvania, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Vissuto a Derry, Westmoreland, Pennsylvania, Stati Uniti d'America nel 1850 e nel Derry Township, Westmoreland, Pennsylvania, Stati Uniti d'America nel 1860. La sua occupazione è indicata come cooper nel Westmoreland, Pennsylvania, Stati Uniti d'America. Morì nel 1863, nel Youngstown, Westmoreland, Pennsylvania, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 72 anni, e fu sepolto a Westmoreland, Pennsylvania, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Johannes Conrad Mooreが1791年10月27日にドイツバイエルンプファルズで生まれた時，父Joh. Daniel Mohrは24歳，母Anna Maria Nagelinは21歳であった。1823年3月2日にアメリカ合衆国ペンシルベニア州レバノンジョーネストウンでSussanna Elisabetha Nagellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1850年にはアメリカ合衆国ペンシルベニア州ウエストモーエランドデリーに，1860年にはアメリカ合衆国ペンシルベニア州ウエストモーエランドデリー トウンシュイップに住んでいた。アメリカ合衆国ペンシルベニア州ウエストモーエランドでの職業はcooperと記録されている。彼は1863年にアメリカ合衆国ペンシルベニア州ウエストモーエランドイオウングストウンで72歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国ペンシルベニア州ウエストモーエランドにて埋葬された。

ko.Longbridge, Westmoreland, Pennsylvania,: 미국 펜실베이니아 주 웨스트모렐랜드 리곤이어 타운시프 론그브리지
ko.Derry Township, Westmoreland, Pennsylvan: 미국 펜실베이니아 주 웨스트모렐랜드 더리 타운시프
ko.Salem Township, Mercer, Pennsylvania, Un: 미국 펜실베이니아 주 머서 살렘 타운시프
ko.lifeSummary: Johannes Conrad Moore(이)가 1791년 10월 27일 독일 베이른 p팔즈에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Joh. Daniel Mohr(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Anna Maria Nagelin(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1823년 3월 2일 미국 펜실베이니아 주 레바넌 조네스타운에서 Sussanna Elisabetha Nagell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1850년도에 미국 펜실베이니아 주 웨스트모렐랜드 더리에서, 1860년도에 미국 펜실베이니아 주 웨스트모렐랜드 더리 타운시프에서 살았다. 그의 직업은 미국 펜실베이니아 주 웨스트모렐랜드에서 cooper이었다. 그는 1863년 미국 펜실베이니아 주 웨스트모렐랜드 영스타운에서 72세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 펜실베이니아 주 웨스트모렐랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Johannes Conrad Moore nasceu em 27 de outubro de 1791, em Pfalz, Bayern, Alemanha, seu pai, Joh. Daniel Mohr, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Anna Maria Nagelin, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Sussanna Elisabetha Nagell em 2 de março de 1823, em Jonestown, Lebanon, Pensilvânia, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele viveu em Derry, Westmoreland, Pensilvânia, Estados Unidos da América em 1850 e em Derry Township, Westmoreland, Pensilvânia, Estados Unidos da América em 1860. Sua profissão aparece como cooper em Westmoreland, Pensilvânia, Estados Unidos da América. Ele faleceu em 1863, em Youngstown, Westmoreland, Pensilvânia, Estados Unidos da América, com 72 anos, e foi sepultado em Westmoreland, Pensilvânia, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Johannes Conrad Moore родился 27 октября 1791 г. в местности пфалз, беиирн, Германия, его отцу по имени Joh. Daniel Mohr было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Anna Maria Nagelin, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Sussanna Elisabetha Nagell 2 марта 1823 г. в местности джоунестоун, лебанон, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он жил в местности дирри, вестмореланд, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты в 1850 году и в местности дирри тоуншип, вестмореланд, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты в 1860 году.  Он работал по профессии cooper в местности вестмореланд, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты. Он умер в 1863 г. в местности енгстоун, вестмореланд, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 72 лет и был похоронен в местности вестмореланд, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Johannes Conrad Moore nació el 27 de octubre de 1791, en Pfalz, Bayern, Alemania, su papá, Joh. Daniel Mohr, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Anna Maria Nagelin, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Sussanna Elisabetha Nagell el 2 de marzo de 1823, en Jonestown, Lebanon, Pensilvania, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Vivió en Derry, Westmoreland, Pensilvania, Estados Unidos en 1850 y en Derry Township, Westmoreland, Pensilvania, Estados Unidos en 1860. Su ocupación registrada era cooper en Westmoreland, Pensilvania, Estados Unidos. Murió en 1863, en Youngstown, Westmoreland, Pensilvania, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 72 años, y fue sepultado en Westmoreland, Pensilvania, Estados Unidos.
